narration: Define a class A with a public void show method that prints 'Hi' 10 times using a for loop.
Define a class B with a public void show method that prints 'Hello' 10 times using a for loop.
Create an object for class A and assign it to a variable obj1.
Create an object for class B and assign it to a variable obj2.
Call the show method on both objects sequentially to verify standard execution.
Explain that execution starts from the main method and creates objects in heap memory sequentially.
Explain that the JVM executes the show method after creating objects, but blocks main until the method completes.
Extend both class A and class B to inherit from the Thread class to make them threads.
Compile and run the code to observe that threads still execute sequentially despite inheritance.
Invoke the start method on both thread objects to initiate execution in parallel.
Explain that calling start creates a new thread and internally calls the run method, similar to starting a race.
Define a class A with a public void show method that prints 'Hi' 10 times using a for loop.
Define a class B with a public void show method that prints 'Hello' 10 times using a for loop.
Create an object for class A and assign it to a variable obj1.
Create an object for class B and assign it to a variable obj2.
Call the show method on both objects sequentially to verify standard execution.
Explain that execution starts from the main method and creates objects in heap memory sequentially.
Explain that the JVM executes the show method after creating objects, but blocks main until the method completes.
Extend both class A and class B to inherit from the Thread class to make them threads.
Compile and run the code to observe that threads still execute sequentially despite inheritance.
Invoke the start method on both thread objects to initiate execution in parallel.
Explain that calling start creates a new thread and internally calls the run method, similar to starting a race.
Increase the loop iterations in the show methods to 100 to make parallel execution more visible.
Explain that the OS scheduler manages thread execution by time-sharing on multi-core CPUs.
Describe how the scheduler allows multiple threads to execute concurrently, interleaving their iterations.
Conclude by summarizing how the Thread class enables parallel execution and mention optimizing output synchronization in the next video.
